mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker containers. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI workflows.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
Install Kind using Go. Ensure you have Go 1.16+ installed. Run the following command to install Kind version v0.31.0:
```bash
go install sigs.k8s.io/kind@v0.31.0
```
If you prefer a binary, download the appropriate binary for your OS:

For Linux (AMD64):
```bash
[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-linux-amd64
chmod +x ./kind
sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind
```

For macOS (Intel):
```bash
[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-darwin-amd64
chmod +x ./kind
mv ./kind /some-dir-in-your-PATH/kind
```

For Windows:
```powershell
curl.exe -Lo kind-windows-amd64.exe https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-windows-amd64
Move-Item .\

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Once Kind is installed, create a Kubernetes cluster with the following command:
```bash
kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0
```
This will create a cluster using Kubernetes version 1.31.0. You can verify the cluster creation by running:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
```

steps.title: Verify Cluster Status
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
```
Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
To enable autoscaling, you can deploy the metrics server:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml
```
Then, enable the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) for your deployments.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
To remove the cluster created by Kind, run:
```bash
kind delete cluster
```
This will clean up all resources associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Kind to a newer version, first uninstall the current version:
```bash
kind delete cluster
```
Then, install the new version:
```bash
go install sigs.k8s.io/kind@v0.31.0
```
Verify the installation:
```bash
kind version
```
Backup any necessary configurations before upgrading.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Fails to Start
troubleshooting.description:
If the cluster fails to start, check Docker's status:
```bash
docker ps -a
```
Ensure Docker is running and has enough resources allocated (CPU, Memory).

troubleshooting.title: kubectl Command Not Found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter 'kubectl: command not found', ensure that kubectl is installed and available in your PATH. Install it using:
```bash
curl -LO "https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
chmod +x ./kubectl
sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl
```

troubleshooting.title: Node Not Ready
troubleshooting.description:
If a node shows 'NotReady', check the logs for the kubelet:
```bash
docker logs <node-name>
```
Look for any errors related to networking or resource allocation.

versionNotes.changes: Improved cluster creation speed and stability.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.
Deprecated certain flags in the CLI.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Kind results in a running local Kubernetes cluster that can be accessed via kubectl. You should be able to deploy applications and test Kubernetes features locally.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running, and Go is installed for Kind installation.
